paper_title: Aerobiological Monitoring and Pollen Analysis in Rzeszów

methods.description: Process for monitoring airborne pollen using a volumetric Hirst type pollen trap to study pollen distribution and concentration in Rzeszów.

steps.description: Aerobiological monitoring was carried out using a volumetric Hirst type pollen trap.
steps.searchable_summary: set up and operate a volumetric Hirst type pollen trap

steps.description: Mugwort pollen grains were counted and identified under 400x magnification from 12 latitudinal transects on each microscope slide.
steps.searchable_summary: count and identify pollen grains under microscope

steps.description: The results were expressed as daily average pollen grains/m3.

methods.description: Method to determine the main pollen season of Artemisia using 95% and 90% methods based on pollen index.

steps.description: The main pollen season of Artemisia was distinguished using 95% and 90% methods.
steps.searchable_summary: determine pollen season using 95% and 90% methods
outputs: pollen season start and end dates

methods.description: Analysis of pollen frequency distribution during the day to identify diurnal patterns and peaks.

steps.description: An arbitrary selection of days was made. Only days without rain were chosen for further analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: select days without rain for analysis

steps.description: Days with more than 20 grains were selected for further analysis.
The pollen counts for each day were calculated as percentages.
The average time of maximum concentration was calculated using circular statistics.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate average time of maximum pollen concentration using circular statistics

methods.description: Statistical analysis to assess the relationship between pollen concentrations and meteorological parameters.

steps.description: The Shapiro-Wilk test was used to check normality.
The Spearman nonparametric correlation test was used to verify the null hypothesis about the lack of correlation between weather parameters and the daily concentration of Artemisia pollen grains.
The angular-linear correlation was calculated to assess the relationship between diurnal pollen concentration and meteorological factors.
